title: GoThru Photographer API GO Project Report

issues.display_title: Issue #35: Fix Issue Photographer Location Endpoint Returns Stale Sublocations (Max 4 Shows Only 3)
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 19/06/26

✅ Implementation Complete — Ready for Unit Testing

Summary

The GET /v1/photographers/{gothruUserId}/location endpoint returns a stale list of sublocations. A photographer can have up to 4 sublocations, but the endpoint keeps returning the previous cached value (e.g. 3) after a new sublocation is added.

Root cause

This is a cache invalidation bug, not a data-retrieval bug.

  • The location controller caches the response under photographer:v*:location:<gothruUserId> (helpers.RedisKey.PhotographerLocation).
  • When a sublocation is added (AddSublocation) or updated (UpdateSublocation), the cache invalidation cleared the detail, geodata, count, and markers patterns — but not the location pattern (PhotographerLocationPattern).
  • Result: the detail endpoint reflects 4 sublocations, while the location endpoint keeps serving the stale cached value (3) until the TTL expires.

The invalidation pattern (PhotographerLocationPattern, photographer:v*:location:<id>) already exists in helpers/redis_keys.go; it was simply never invoked from the sublocation mutations.

Fix

Add PhotographerLocationPattern invalidation, keyed by photographer.GothruUserID (the location cache is keyed by gothru user id only), in both mutation actions:

  • src/action/v1/sublocations/addSublocation.go
  • src/action/v1/sublocations/updateSublocation.go
_, _ = helpers.ClearRedisKeys(helpers.RedisKey.PhotographerLocationPattern(photographer.GothruUserID))

The v* wildcard in the pattern covers all cache versions.

GetPhotographerLocation (action + controller) needs no change — its query is correct (Find with no limit, no soft-delete on the model).

Verification

  • go build ./src/action/v1/sublocations/ passes.
  • Manual: add a 4th sublocation, then call the location endpoint — it now returns 4 immediately instead of the stale 3.

Out of scope (noted, not changed)

The DB column is misspelled latilatitude (mapped in models/directory_photographer_sublocation.go and referenced by raw SQL in getBulkCityPhotographerCounts.go and getPhotographerCount.go). Renaming it requires a DB migration plus updating all references; left as-is to avoid breaking production queries.

Relates to #28, #29.
